title: SUMMER EBT - 211info
text: LA PINE SELF-SUFFICIENCY SUMMER EBT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Summer EBT is a grocery benefits program. It helps families buy food for their school-aged children when school is out. Each summer, families will get one yearly payment of $120 on an EBT card for each eligible child to buy groceries. Unused Summer EBT benefits expire 122 days from the date of issuance. The helpline is available for general questions, eligibility confirmation, application status and EBT card questions. 16493 Bluewood Place Suite 1 La Pine, OR 97739 Eligibility: Families may automatically receive Summer EBT if: Participating child was approved for Summer EBT benefits through an approved application OR Participating children attend a school that participates in the National School Lunch Program (NSLP) or School Breakfast Program (SBP) and qualify based on household income SNAP allotment greater than $0, Medicaid programs with income eligibility at or below 185% of the Federal Poverty level based on household size Families may need to apply to be eligible if: Household meets the income requirements for free or reduced-price meals at school, and the child attends a school that participates in the National School Lunch Program (NSLP) or School Breakfast Program (SBP) Hours: Call Center will reopen May 2026 Email Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Apply for 2026 benefits anytime by completing an application FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Families can apply anytime for the following year by submitting an application Applications can be sent via mail or email LANGUAGES: Online and paper application available in English, Spanish, Somali, Chuukese, Russian, Arabic, Vietnamese, Traditional Chinese, Simplified Chinese, and Ukrainian AREA SERVED: Deschutes County Services: Food Stamps/SNAP Summer Food Service Programs * Food Stamps/SNAP Recipients Other Services Offered At This Location: PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - EMPLOYMENT RELATED DAYCARE PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - SNAP PROGRAM PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE TO DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SURVIVORS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. LA PINE SELF-SUFFICIENCY
